issue_title: How do you update a custom user storage provider jar that includes a version number?
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

core

### Describe the bug

I have a User Storage Provider jar that I wrote to use our corporate sign in databse from keycloak which I wrote when using keycloak 19.0.2. I have since updated keycloak to 23.0.4 and had to update the user storage provider jar. When I did so, I updated the version number, which is also in the name of the jar.

However, I am now unable to use the new jar as keycloak's build process complains that the old jar is not found and crashes :(

I have had to symlink the new jar to the old name to get keycloak to build and start, how do I get around this limitation?

Note: I have been using the Quarkus version for 19.0.2 and, of course, for 23.0.4

### Version

23.0.4

### Expected behavior

I should be able to replace the old custom provider jar in the providers directory with the new version without having to symlink the new jar file to have the old name :(

### Actual behavior

keycloak will not build or start when you update a jar in providers (a user storage provider in this case) by deleting the old version and adding the new version. You have to symlink , or rename, the new jar to have the same name as the old version :(

### How to Reproduce?

1. Have a user storage provider with a version in the name, such as keycloak-user-store-1.0.0-SNAPSHOT.jar.
2. Build and start the keycloak server
3. Shut down the keycloak server.
4. Then attempt to update the user storage provider to a new version with a different version number, such as keycloak-user-store-2.0.0-SNAPSHOT.jar, and delete the old keycloak-user-store-1.0.0-SNAPSHOT.jar
5. Start the server

### Anything else?

_No response_

issue_title: Remove support for old cookie path
issue_body:
### Description

In early versions of Keycloak cookies wouldn't have a trailing `/` in the path, so the cookie path would be `/auth/realms/master`.

This introduced some issues with IE11 specifically as it was wrongly sending cookies for `/auth/realms/master` to `/auth/realms/mastersomething`.  For more details see https://issues.redhat.com/browse/KEYCLOAK-5270

The solution to this issue was to add a `/` to the end of the cookie path, making it `/auth/realms/master/`. For backwards compatibility reasons there is currently code that expires/removes any old cookies without the `/` at the end, but since this has not been the case since before Keycloak 4 this backwards compatibility code can be removed. 

### Discussion

_No response_

### Motivation

Cleanup cookie handling code 

### Details

_No response_

issue_title: NullPointerException when using client scope policy for token-exchange
commit_message:
Fix scope policy evaluation for client to client token exchange (#26435)

Previously the scope from the token was not set available in the ClientModelIdentity attributes.
This caused the NPE in `org.keycloak.authorization.policy.provider.clientscope.ClientScopePolicyProvider.hasClientScope`(..)
when calling `identity.getAttributes().getValue("scope")`.

We now pass the provided decoded AccessToken down to the ClientModelIdentity creation
to allow to populate the required scope attribute.

We also ensure backwards compatibility for ClientPermissionManagement API.

Fixes #26435

Signed-off-by: Thomas Darimont <thomas.darimont@googlemail.com>

issue_title: Incorrect position of nonce in OCSP request
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

authentication

### Describe the bug

The nonce of the OCSP request sent by Keycloak is different from RFC 6960. 
According to RFC 6960, nonces should be sent as requestExtensions, but Keycloak does not.

https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc6960#section-4.1
https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc6960#section-4.4.1 

Therefore, for example, when using OpenSSL as an OCSP responder, 
the nonce is not recognized correctly and the nonce value is not returned in the OCSP response.

Below is the location of the nonce when capturing OCSP request packets using Keycloak and OpenSSL.

Case: Keycloak OCSP request(different from RFC6960)
----------------------------------------------------------------------------
- tbsRequest
  - requestList
    - reqCert
    - singleRequestExtensions
      - Extension(nonce) <-- NG

Case: OpenSSL OCSP client(matches RFC6960)
----------------------------------------------------------------------------
- tbsRequest
  - requestList
    - reqCert
  - requestExtensions
    - Extension(nonce) <-- OK


### Version

23.0.4

### Expected behavior

OCSP response includes nonce

### Actual behavior

OCSP response does not include nonce

### How to Reproduce?

1. Config x509 authentication to the authentication flow according to https://www.keycloak.org/docs/latest/server_admin/index.html#_x509.
2. Set "OCSP Checking Enabled" to "ON" and "OCSP Responder URI" to "http://localhost:8088"
3. Start OCSP responder on port 8088 with openssl
`
openssl ocsp -index {index.txt file path} -rsigner {CA certificate path} -rkey {CA private key path} -port 8088 -CA {CA certificate path} -text
`
4. Execute X509 certificate authentication
5. If you capture a packet from port 8088, you will see that no nonce is being sent in the OCSP response.

### Anything else?

In addition to the above, when looking at the OCSP request with wireshark, it appears that only the nonce value is not output correctly and is recognized as an invalid packet. There seems to be another problem with how the nonce value is stored.

Also, related to this issue, the nonce check is not performed if the OCSP response does not contain a nonce, so it is difficult to notice the problem of missing nonce. You may need to consider making nonce checking mandatory as an option, or issuing a warning log.
commit_message:
Fix OCSP nonce handling

Closes #26439

Co-authored-by: Ricardo Martin <rmartinc@redhat.com>
Signed-off-by: Justin Tay <49700559+justin-tay@users.noreply.github.com>

issue_title: "Test connection" on LDAPS URI does not test TLS handshake
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

admin/ui

### Describe the bug

When creating an LDAP federation in the web interface, there is a `Test Connection` button. However, this button only checks for a TCP handshake and tests neither the transport layer (TLS) nor the application layer. This can be seen when watching a packet capture on the backend LDAP server; whether using LDAP, LDAP with StartTLS, or LDAPS, keycloak only initiates a TCP handshake and then ends with a FIN ACK.

As a result, `Test Connection` will incorrectly report a functioning connection over URIs that would obviously fail at the transport level, such `ldaps://localhost:8080` (the keycloak HTTP interface).

Further, the `Test Authentication` button operates inconsistently. When a valid user is provided, it attempts to perform a handshake on TCP, security layer, and LDAP-- unless the settings are StartTLS with Truststore SPI set to Always, in which case it only performs a TCP handshake and disconnects.

### Version

22.0.5

### Expected behavior

1. `Test Connection` should validate TLS and StartTLS settings as part of the connection.
	- To confirm connectivity up to the application level, an anonymous LDAP bind should be attempted. Any LDAP response should be considered a success.
2. `Test Authentication` with bind type `none` should attempt an anonymous bind
3. `Test Authentication` should always attempt an LDAP bind and should always respect TLS / StartTLS settings

### Actual behavior

1. `Test Connection` only performs a TCP handshake and ignores TLS and startTLS settings
2. `Test Authentication` with bind type `none` only attempts a TCP connect, disregarding TLS settings
3. `Test Authentication` with bind type `simple`, StartTLS on and Truststore SPI set to Always only attempts a TCP connect and does not respect StartTLS settings or attempt a bind

### How to Reproduce?

In my testing, I did not set up trust for the LDAP TLS CA. This is not necessary as the incorrect behavior occurs before TLS verification would occur, and the lack of trust makes it easier to see in the Web UI what is actually happening.

| Environment | . |
| ---- | ---- |
| Keycloak Version | 22.05 (docker) |
| Keycloak Database | Postgres15 (docker) |
| LDAP Hostname | ldap.lab.local |
| Keycloak Hostname | keycloak.lab.local |
| Other | Keycloak does NOT trust LDAPS CA |

 1. Install and run a live packet capture on the LDAP server with filter 'host keycloak.domain.local'
 2. Verify normal packet patterns for ldap connection:

| Test | Command | Packet Capture |
| ---- | ---- | ---- |
| Plain LDAP | `ldapsearch -H ldap://ldap.lab.local:389 -s oneLevel "(cn=conn_test)"` | TCP handshake, LDAP response |
| LDAPS wrong port | `ldapsearch -H ldaps://ldap.lab.local:389 -s oneLevel "(cn=conn_test)"` | TCP Handshake, TLS handshake failure |
| LDAP StartTLS | `ldapsearch -Z -H ldap://ldap.lab.local:389 -s oneLevel "(cn=conn_test)"` | TCP Handshake, StartTLS issued |
| LDAPS correct port | `ldapsearch -H ldaps://ldap.lab.local:636 -s oneLevel "(cn=conn_test)"` | TCP Handshake, TLS handshake |

3. Create a new realm, and begin creating a new LDAP federation. Set Bind Type to 'none' and run both `Test Connection` and `Test Authentication`:

| Test               | URL | StartTLS                                                                  | Keycloak UI result    | Packet Capture                          |
| ------------------ | --- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------- | --- | ------------------------------- |
| Plain LDAP         | ldap://ldap.lab.local:389    | false                                                                          | Successfully connected to LDAP    | TCP handshake    |
| LDAPS wrong port   | ldaps://ldap.lab.local:389    | false   | Successfully connected to LDAP    | TCP handshake      |
| LDAP StartTLS      | ldap://ldap.lab.local:389    | True | Error when trying to connect to LDAP: 'UnknownError'    | TCP handshake |
| LDAPS correct port | ldaps://ldap.lab.local:636    | False   | Successfully connected to LDAP    | TCP handshake    |

4. Change Authentication type to 'simple' and provide a valid LDAP user. Test the `Test Authentication` button.

| Test | URL | StartTLS | Trust SPI | Keycloak UI result | Packet Capture |
| ---- | ---- | ---- | ---- | ---- | ---- |
| Plain LDAP | ldap://ldap.lab.local:389 | false | Any | Successfully connected to LDAP | TCP handshake, LDAP query |
| LDAPS wrong port | ldaps://ldap.lab.local:389 | false | Any | 'Socket Reset' | TCP handshake, TLS Handshake |
| LDAP StartTLS SPI Always | ldap://ldap.lab.local:389 | True | Always | 'UnknownError' | TCP handshake |
| LDAP StartTLS SPI Never | ldap://ldap.lab.local:389 | True | Never | 'SSL Handshake Failed' | TCP handshake, StartTLS |
| LDAPS correct port | ldaps://ldap.lab.local:636 | False | Any | 'SSL Handshake Failed' | TCP handshake, TLS handshake |


### Anything else?

The positioning of the `Test Connection` button is problematic because it's position below the StartTLS and TrustStore settings imply that it will test those settings as well, but it does not.

The fact that these buttons exist but perform inconsistently makes troubleshooting exceptionally difficult because there is no single way to validate only the lower layers and then validate only the authentication settings.

EDITED to add numbering on expected / actuals to simplify referencing the pieces of this issue.

issue_title: SPI-truststore-file-type option appears to be invalid
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

docs

### Describe the bug

The documentation ([Keycloak](https://www.keycloak.org/server/keycloak-truststore), [Red Hat](https://access.redhat.com/documentation/en-us/red_hat_build_of_keycloak/22.0/html/server_guide/keycloak-truststore-)) references a `spi-truststore-file-type` option:

```
spi-truststore-file-type
The type of truststore, such as jks, pkcs12 or bcfks. If not provided, the type would be detected based on the truststore file extension or platform default type.
```
However, none of the examples use this option, and a [repository code search](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3Akeycloak%2Fkeycloak%20spi-truststore-file&type=code) for `spi-truststore-file` shows that the only options referenced by code are:

 - spi-truststore-file-file
 - spi-truststore-file-password
 - spi-truststore-file-hostname-verification-policy


### Version

N/A

### Expected behavior

The documentation should only reference valid options. If the type option is valid, an example should be provided showing it in use. If only jks keystores are supported, that should be made clear.

Additionally, the documentation does not indicate whether these SPI options are supported by environmental variables in container deployments (e.g. `KC_SPI_TRUSTSTORE_FILE_FILE`).

### Actual behavior

The documentation apparently does not match the implementation.

### How to Reproduce?

See https://github.com/search?q=repo%3Akeycloak%2Fkeycloak%20spi-truststore-file&type=code.

### Anything else?

The SPI truststore options are [apparently being deprecated in 24.x](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/d23383e79ccf0604e72d627cd6a03893b1bfcc77/docs/documentation/upgrading/topics/keycloak/changes-24_0_0.adoc#truststore-changes), however this is still relevant as it affects the current version.

Additionally, that change note references the documentation link above, which as of [current main](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/main/docs/guides/server/keycloak-truststore.adoc) does not indicate whether a type option is necessary or exists.

issue_title: The way CRLs are currently loaded is slow and uses large amounts of memory
issue_body:
### Description

The CRLs should be cached for speed, as it take take several seconds and a large amount of memory and CPU usage for every single user to log in when CRLs are being used. The larger the CRLs get, the worse the problem gets. For CRLs like the ones found here ( https://crl.gds.disa.mil/ ) Keycloak can crash out of memory and can take up to 5 or 6 seconds for every login attempt to complete.
Also, CRLs change over time and should be redownloaded at a particular interval defined by the CRL itself. Right now this doesn't happen and an admin would need to manually manage the CRLs being used.

### Discussion

_No response_

### Motivation

With large enough CRLs or simply enough of them configured, Keycloak becomes unusable.

### Details

_No response_

issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

### Area

storage

### Describe the bug

As title said,
failed to link the user with an existing read-token role from the federation provider when AddReadTokenRoleOnCreate was enabled for the IdP.

The error logs are like
```
org.hibernate.exception.ConstraintViolationException: could not execute statement [Unique index or primary key violation: "PUBLIC.PRIMARY_KEY_41 ON PUBLIC.FED_USER_ROLE_MAPPING(ROLE_ID, USER_ID) VALUES ( /* key:1 */ 'f7977142-8b66-428a-9941-d473a9d43771', 'f:3ca9bed7-f7c3-49ba-bfc6-d6faf45e82fb:testuser')"; SQL statement:
insert into FED_USER_ROLE_MAPPING (REALM_ID,STORAGE_PROVIDER_ID,ROLE_ID,USER_ID) values (?,?,?,?) [23505-224]] [insert into FED_USER_ROLE_MAPPING (REALM_ID,STORAGE_PROVIDER_ID,ROLE_ID,USER_ID) values (?,?,?,?)]
```

### Version

nightly

### Expected behavior

Link success

### Actual behavior

Error on first broker login

### How to Reproduce?

1. Setup the IdP with `AddReadTokenRoleOnCreate` enabled
2. Setup a federation provider
3. Create a user and grant `broker.read-token` role with the user into the federation storage
4. Login from the IdP to link to the user

The JPA `UserAdapter` has check the role before granting.
https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/28c9f98930e38852e23ffbdb7b4cd7546efe46f8/model/jpa/src/main/java/org/keycloak/models/jpa/UserAdapter.java#L452-L455

https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/28c9f98930e38852e23ffbdb7b4cd7546efe46f8/model/legacy/src/main/java/org/keycloak/storage/adapter/AbstractUserAdapterFederatedStorage.java#L179-L181

issue_title: Post Quarkus 3.7 dependency alignment
issue_body:
A breakdown of dependency issues following the quarkus upgrade:

Versions where we trail quarkus - may need individual issues:
- bc-fips to 1.0.2.4 - exhibited classloading problems
- liquibase to 4.25.1 - even after updating CustomLockService, there is still a test failure 
- osgi to 6.0 - didn't build successfully

Versions where we are ahead of quarkus, and that are used for more than tests:
- jakarata.mail - quarkus is actually still using com.sun.mail, rather than jakarta.mail https://github.com/quarkusio/quarkus/issues/26989

Versions where we are even with quarkus, but have our own property:
- slf4j - used only for an additional test dependency not covered by the quarkus bom
- infinispan - used for more than tests and need the version to import the infinispan bom

Versions where we are trail quarkus, and that are used for more than tests, but seem to upgrade without an issue:
- resteasy
- wildfly-elytron

And a lot of test related dependencies / overlap - mostly drivers, but also awaitility, hamcrest, and testcontainers. Driver versions cannot be removed because we reference them as variables in our tests to switch between databases.

_Originally posted by @shawkins in https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/pull/26203#discussion_r1467792077_

issue_title: Mysterious settings changes due to Keycloak cluster changes
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

core

### Describe the bug

The other day we had an issue with our Keycloak cluster. We're running 3 nodes hosted in Azure App Service. At one point one of the nodes was recycled and a new node was started to replace it. During the shutdown/startup we can see from the logs that there were some issues with the Infinispan cache (`java.lang.IllegalStateException: Read commands must ignore leavers` and timeouts).

But, as it seems the nodes were back in normal operation soon after, as expected. What follows is not as expected though. Suddenly we're seeing an increased number of expired refresh tokens and consequently 401s.

By more or less coincidence we saw that the `Offline Session Idle` setting was configured to 42 minutes and not 42 days as we had configured it. Changing this value back to days (not minutes) immediately resolved the expired refresh token issue.

So to us it seems like this setting was changed somehow due to the recycling of the node and/or the Infinispan issues. 


### Version

22.0.1

### Expected behavior

The `Offline Session Idle` setting (and all other settings) should not be affected by changes to the Keycloak cluster.

### Actual behavior

Seems like the the `Offline Session Idle` setting was changed due changes in the Keycloak cluster.

### How to Reproduce?

N/A.

### Anything else?

_No response_

issue_title: Issues related to IDNHomographValidator
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

user-profile

### Describe the bug

There are few issues related to `IDNHomographValidator` :

- When I try to add user with username `你好世界` to some realm in the admin console, it is not allowed (correct behaviour as it should not be allowed due the homograph validatory), but no error message is displayed to the administrator. So administrator does't know what happened and why user cannot be created. This is due the fact that error message is incorrectly constructed based on the `value` instead of `inputHint` like used by other validators.

- The `IDNHomographValidator` does not implement `ConfiguredProvider` . This means that:
  - If validator is accidentally removed from the `username` field, it cannot be easily added back (unless admin explicitly edit JSON, which is not user-friendly)
  - Validator cannot be easily added to any other fields (with the exception of manual JSON edit as mentioned in previous point). But this validator might be useful for some additional fields, not just for username.

### Version

latest main

### Expected behavior

Error message is displayed to the admin/user in case of homograph validator error. And it is possible to add validator to fields in user-profile configuration

### Actual behavior

Error message is NOT displayed to the admin/user in case of homograph validator error. And it is NOT possible to add validator to fields in user-profile configuration

### How to Reproduce?

See description

### Anything else?

_No response_

### Describe the bug

With f44cda262124ec76737386717f53a84dd956b84a for #4978 `useNonce` parameter was added.

I'm checking keycloak.js with [Spring Authorization Server](https://docs.spring.io/spring-authorization-server) and almost everything works. But...

my authorization request is:
```
http://localhost:9000/oauth2/authorize?
    client_id=hawtio-public&

```
It is successful with this redirect:
```

    code=vNVS6qV-sUefkPqM1dPm1aq7v1_JyJ4-U1eUug0E94e6m6Uze8TQuz7SL73N5I0Y7Glk71--CFmEhTg3rrKUVnN58OL8w0L1pQr_KHI0vkx194r2mwy_M190aAsFQkkV&
    state=ac99f440-7953-4f1a-8536-1854d1a4d3be
```
Then token endpoint is POSTed with:
```
code=vNVS6qV-sUefkPqM1dPm1aq7v1_JyJ4-U1eUug0E94e6m6Uze8TQuz7SL73N5I0Y7Glk71--CFmEhTg3rrKUVnN58OL8w0L1pQr_KHI0vkx194r2mwy_M190aAsFQkkV&
grant_type=authorization_code&

code_verifier=iPekAWjAJ9OMBzRAJi9PHvFyFSsoixIXsED8rAGufFSqtRFdwQaGEEZWsHdUCEXv2Gzo01go1eBkWBPgxeAmH4rOEJHorizd
```
However the response contains (item [1] of JTWs is base64 decoded):
```
{
"access_token":"
    eyJraWQiOiI3MThmYjYwOS00MGQ4LTRiMjYtYmUyZC1mY2I3NGM3OTU1YzgiLCJhbGciOiJSUzI1NiJ9
    {
      "sub": "user1",
      "aud": "hawtio-public",
      "nbf": 1706709324,
      "scope": [
        "openid"
      ],
      "iss": "http://localhost:9000",
      "exp": 1706709624,
      "iat": 1706709324,
      "jti": "079aab32-9799-4e73-ae76-3b9d61c51992"
    }
    H1b8lWu18VZEOM-oyNdjImFg_0ELEXEfjxavFrdR-6Rurz4MDKnyRTLXg2Oemhfpbm4xIGPfi8rwOJn7Kc-VT8BqD5U_AOslRyje9Hf6k7TnTOId32D-Nn_YoWU2EUWQAZSyDGdXotHLiAsIedB27e5CWx25BambZ-mil4TieYoXjiVrOOSITMi6fkdECyat8cdOZiiXc4FzCNygGcsz1jHCOlUDN39W5Y1r_wRUhK2O1UnfxcAAI6zRsc2z_ocu4vRiBXf979LfY6e5JSe31JnWBOrp9m4udMnmgUEf838-RG0-D_obAvC5zyX6WGy3BpnHzyIl9lhmHVh3Q0eSEw",
"scope":"openid",
"id_token":"
    eyJraWQiOiI3MThmYjYwOS00MGQ4LTRiMjYtYmUyZC1mY2I3NGM3OTU1YzgiLCJhbGciOiJSUzI1NiJ9
    {
      "sub": "user1",
      "aud": "hawtio-public",
      "azp": "hawtio-public",
      "auth_time": 1706708739,
      "iss": "http://localhost:9000",
      "exp": 1706711124,
      "iat": 1706709324,
      "nonce": "dd31a03c-d7e0-4771-959c-a3c39792bb84",
      "jti": "34984761-de7c-414a-9506-e7c6dfdea88e",
      "sid": "IhCpYEA4R0V_LPS3OYyIuplpxhYK12wNl-R4NgLuXQo"
    }
    fNTowFiRgZqNpUh6ut5HlUAxRvALEptkW1cxuwNpdrXutWpV1hxs8mVzwsVzWljp-Z6blq0ba_X-aoC3KnoibQr8Dr3HnFDP7rQtNURxInZjJwbxJmJWGwDy8zwEVbYSMpPtDvThf0ffWP9IrsJgqD1kQBxKx6eXgxe19uhFJHuhdmM1ZH6We0U-BVUn-kYmKN27e_V3077OPW3tgNqRz7F6Ftc8-j2HtYB7JZANkDWGZc3HRNgbDRHpSflRsdeO77t1rYBsPZRku7y1zkpoTYLRGTP-hknSatn9I2ulWkZB5738aXAX6pYTeI1PR-Yk5K9R7tqKcJOap880aeHQ8w",
"token_type":"Bearer",
"expires_in":299
}
```
So:

* `id_token` contains the nonce (according to https://openid.net/specs/openid-connect-core-1_0.html#IDToken)
* `access_token` doesn't contain the nonce (OAuth 2 doesn't define the content of tokens and RFC 7519 (JWT) doesn't mention nonce as well)

Can the nonce be mandatory only for id_token, but not for access_token and refresh_token?

### Version

23.0.5

### Expected behavior

`nonce` claim not available in `access_token` and not required.

### Actual behavior

`nonce` claim not available in `access_token` but required by keycloak.js.

### How to Reproduce?

Use keycloak.js with Spring Authorization Server

### Anything else?

_No response_

issue_title: Keycloak and MSAD: enabling account in MSAD does not propagate to Keycloak
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

ldap

### Describe the bug

### Original report

If User Federation's "Edit Mode" is "WRITABLE", 
when an admin makes an Keycloak account disabled, the corresponding AD account becomes disabled.
Then, the admin make the AD account enabled again from AD side, the Keycloak account left disabled even when the "Synchronize all users" is executed.

Customers expects "Edit Mode"=WRITABLE syncs Keycloak user storage and LDAP server, but this behavior is annoying.

### Additional notes

When looking at `MSADUserAccountControlStorageMapper.isEnabled()` , it is clear that in case the account disabled in Keycloak DB, it will always takes precedence - https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/23.0.5/federation/ldap/src/main/java/org/keycloak/storage/ldap/mappers/msad/MSADUserAccountControlStorageMapper.java#L238-L248 . We may need to check whether to revisit this behaviour for the use-case mentioned by customer above.

Not sure if we need some "compatibility switch" on the MSAD mapper to achieve the backwards compatibility or if we can just switch to the behaviour above? There is possible option that we will have switch to always rely just on the "enabled" state from MSAD (so never call `super.setEnabled` or `super.isEnabled` ), which will make it easier to support backwards compatibility if needed...

### Version

23.0.5

### Expected behavior

See above

### Actual behavior

See above

### How to Reproduce?

See above

### Anything else?

_No response_
commit_message:
Align isEnabled in MSAD mappers to how other properties are processed in UserAttributeLDAPStorageMapper

- user model is updated by onImport with the enabled/disabled status of the LDAP user
- a config option always.read.enabled.value.from.ldap was introduced, in synch to what we have in UserAttributeLDAPStorageMapper
- isEnabled checks the flag to decide if it should always retrieve the value from LDAP, or return the local value.
- setEnabled first updates the LDAP tx, and then calls the delegate to avoid issue #24201

Closes #26695
Closed #24201

Signed-off-by: Stefan Guilhen <sguilhen@redhat.com>

issue_title: Empty error message when validation issue due the PersonNameProhibitedValidator validation
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

user-profile

### Describe the bug

- Edit some attribute in admin console in user-profile and go to section `Validations`

- Try to add validator `person-name-prohibited-characters`

- There is configuration option `Error message key`, which has this tooltip `Key of the error message in i18n bundle. Dafault message key is error-person-name-invalid-character` . This implies to me that I can just leave this empty to have the default name.

- **Issue 1**: There is minor typo in the tooltip (Dafault)

- I save the validator. In the JSON, it is saved with empty `error-message` option.

```
    {
      "name": "my-attribute",
      "displayName": "",
      "validations": {
        "person-name-prohibited-characters": {
          "error-message": ""
        }
      },
```

- **Issue 2:** Now I try to edit some user and I fill `my-attribute` with incorrect value like `Jo&hn` . There is only error message `my-attribute` without any further explanation of what was wrong. Reason is, that default error message was not applied, but instead, it used empty error message. See screenshot

![Screenshot from 2024-02-02 17-29-50](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/assets/1223965/4ad158e6-cd3f-47ab-81b7-4e1f332cdad9)


NOTE: Same issue happens also for some other validators (like `PatternValidator` or `UsernameProhibitedCharactersValidator`)

### Version

nightly

### Expected behavior

Some more appropriate error message like `my attribute has invalid characters` instead of just `my attribute` without any explanation. Basically when "error message" parameter is not null, but blank, it can be still good to fallback to default error message.

### Actual behavior

Error message `my-attribute` without any explanation (See screenshot)

### How to Reproduce?

See above

### Anything else?

Not sure if this can be improved also in the UI layer and when attempting to save validator with some empty configuration parameter, it is considered that this configuration parameter was not used at all (rather than adding this parameter with empty value). So in my case, it can just save validator without error-message parameter like:
```
      "validations": {
        "person-name-prohibited-characters": {}
      },
```
instead of:
```
      "validations": {
        "person-name-prohibited-characters": {
          "error-message": ""
        }
      },
```


This would need to be fixed in UI layer though...

issue_title: Maximum 100 resources with same URI checked when requesting permissions by URI
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

authorization-services

### Describe the bug

When requesting resource permissions via the token endpoint  with `permission_resource_format=uri` only a maximum of 100 resources with the same URI are evaluated and returned. This is a fix value and not effected when setting the `response_permissions_limit`  because  AuthorizationTokenService.getResourceListByUri uses Constants.DEFAULT_MAX_RESULTS for searching in the resource store.

https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/3f07a9522e28a746515c1fe8bdce9634e5d00763/services/src/main/java/org/keycloak/authorization/authorization/AuthorizationTokenService.java#L893-L894

### Version

23.0.5

### Expected behavior

`AuthorizationTokenService.getResourceListByUri` should use a customizable limit for searching in the resource store. For example `response_permissions_limit` when called via the token endpoint.  

### Actual behavior

At the moment `AuthorizationTokenService.getResourceListByUri` uses always `Constants.DEFAULT_MAX_RESULTS`

### How to Reproduce?

Here is a short example how we constructed our resource URIs and ran into the issue.
In this example there are multiple tenants that have resources ( Documents ) and users that have access to some of the documents of a tenant. We want to use URIs to check permission for a single resources and to obtain a list of all resources a user has access to.

We added two URIs to every resource.
```
"/tenant-1/documents/UUID"  ( to check the single resource)
"/tenant-1/documents" ( to get a list of all resources a user of tenant-1 has access )
```

Now if there are more than 100 resources with URI `/tenant-1/documents`. It is not possible to check permissions for the resources 101-xxx when requesting permissions with following curl request.

```
curl -X POST \
https://$KEYCLOAK/realms/$REALM/protocol/openid-connect/token \
-H "Authorization: Bearer $TOKEN" \
--data "grant_type=urn:ietf:params:oauth:grant-type:uma-ticket" \
--data "audience=$CLIENT" \
--data "permission_resource_format=uri" \
--data "permission=/tenant-1/documents" \
--data "response_permissions_limit=1000" \
--data "response_mode=permissions"
```

### Anything else?

Regardless of the limitation of the same URI to a maximum of 100 occurrences.
I don't think we are the only ones who want to retrieve a list of all permissions for a particular resource type or URI such as "/tenant-1/documents".  So if there is an obvious problem with our approach of using URIs to achieve this functionality, we would be grateful for a hint on how to improve this.
commit_message:
Use the response_permissions_limit value, if provided, to set the maximum number of results when retrieving resources by URI

Signed-off-by: BrunoSampaioDTx <bruno.sampaio@dtx-colab.pt>
